comments.body: What URL are you using trying to hit your app?
Also, when i hit the above url in console i can see the following lines :
<24 Oct, 2013 3:40:45 AM IST> <Warning> <Socket> <BEA-000449> <Closing the socket, as no data read from it on 0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1:54,420 during the configured idle timeout of 5 seconds.>

answers.body: Please review the following documentation from Oracle: http://www.oracle.com/webfolder/technetwork/tutorials/obe/fmw/wls/12c/03-DeployApps/deployapps.htm

This is very common issue with weblogic server. All you have to do is to "Configure the Listen Address for every server"

Login to console and go to environment>server>admin/managed server Under configuration>general Tab, you will find "Listen Address".

Provide the IP or host anme in this field for admin as well as every managed server.

and its done

I checked several similar situations, and the BEA-170011 appears when the AdminServer isn't responding, thus it's just a consequence.

After this warning comes up, the JVM could crash, it could get stuck or an OOM could be produced, between other incidents.

Note that in this particular case, the server you are trying to start is the admin server.

Don't worry about BEA-170011, the right question is, Why isn't the AdminServer responding?

There you may have several options.

  • if the JVM ended up in a crash, check the hs_err_pid log file for tips (in Linux based environments).
  • if an OOM was produced with a heap dump file. Analyze the heap dump file with Eclipse MAT.
  • if an OOM was produced without a heap dump file, configure the JVM to produce one under OOM condition, using these JVM options in the DOMAIN/setDomainEnv.sh: -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError -XX:HeapDumpPath=HeapDumpOOMR_[SERVER_NAME].hprof
  • if after that warning, the server just gets freeze, take a thread dump (C:\Oracle\MIDDLE~1\JDK160~1\bin\jstack [pid]) to know which thread got stuck. That will give you more tips.
  • corruption at the embeddedLDAP.
  • SSL misconfiguration (not for your particular case) You can try increasing the heap (for this example 512m was used --> -Xmx512m).

As you can see, the BEA-170011 is just the beginning of an adventure.

title: Using Weblogic 12c, giving warning Warning Log Management BEA-170011 The LogBroadcaster on this server failed to
body:
I am using weblogic 12c server to deploy my web applications. These were running fine but now it has started showing the following warning : - 

<Warning> <Log Management> <BEA-170011> 
<The LogBroadcaster on this server failed to broadcast log messages to the Administration Server. 
The Administration Server may not be running. Message broadcasts to the Administration Server will be disabled.>

I am not able to see even home page of my web application. Please help me, I am a java programmer having very limited knowledge in weblogic admin. The full stack trace from console is given below: -
